original_abstract: This research explores the implementation of the Religious Program and Deepening of Scriptural Books (AKPK) at SMPN 1 Darangdan, a policy initiated by the Purwakarta Regency to address the lack of religious literacy and prevent intolerance among students. Using a qualitative descriptive approach, data were gathered through observations, interviews, and documentation to evaluate the program’s management and its impact on student values. The findings reveal that the AKPK program is systematically managed through the POAC (Planning, Organizing, Actuating, and Controlling) framework. This includes strategic scheduling of sessions before regular classes, designating specific monitoring teams, and integrating practical worship such as the Duha prayer. Supporting factors identified include the availability of digital and physical guidebooks, strong managerial leadership, and active teacher participation. Conversely, challenges such as time constraints and varying levels of Arabic literacy among students were noted, with the school response being the addition of specialized instructional hours. Ultimately, the program strengthens students' spiritual and social values, fostering improved relationships with the Divine and the community while promoting a moderate understanding of religious teachings to minimize social friction.
humanized_abstract: This study aimed to analyze the implementation of Religious Program and Deepening of Scriptural Books (AKPK) policy in SMPN 1 Darangdan, an educational institution which located in Purwakarta Regency that is designed to meet the needs of the students’ religious literacy and to avoid religious intolerance. This study uses a qualitative descriptive approach by collecting the data through observations, interviews, and documents analysis to determine the management and the implications of the implementation of AKPK towards the students’ values. The results of this study indicated that the implementation of AKPK policy in SMPN 1 Darangdan is organized using a form of planning, organizing, actuating, and controlling (POAC). This policy consists of the preparedness planning prior to the beginning of the regular classes, the task assignments given to several parts of schools organization to carry out the worship activities such as Duha prayer, and other supporting tools utilized by students, teachers, and schools managers. The successful implementation of AKPK policy is also reinforced by several instruments, including the use of the digital and print versions of the guidebooks, the management accountability given to the manager, and the active participation of all teachers in implementing the AKPK policy. This study further found the obstacles faced by schools in implementing this policy, namely time allocation and the Arabic fluency difficulties faced by students. The school responded to this challenge by adding an additional learning hour as special lesson aimed at dealing with the Arabic literacy difficulties faced by students. The implementation of AKPK policy activities is shown to develop the students’ spiritual and social values so that they become better individuals in their interactions with the Almighty as well as with the society around them and at the same time helps to curb potential social unrest arising from religious intolerance.
